reviews.review_msg: Super disorganized and the lectures are sometimes 100+ slides with no particular order. She just randomly throws theories to you without connecting them or explaining their significance. Easy class, horrible teacher. Finished with an A but still learned nothing.

Initially gave Meg the benefit of the doubt, but found reviews were very accurate. She rushes through 150+ slides or goes off-topic, making lectures confusing. She and TAs rarely respond on Canvas, and slides are ALWAYS uploaded late. Even her study guide didn't help—MC questions were oddly worded. Avoid this class unless the structure improves.

Overall, she is a very decent instructor and is passionate about the materials she teaches. However, she did miss a lot of classes due to her sick days. But when she is available, she did a great job introducing many concepts that turned out to be useful for AI research.

I really enjoyed COGS100 with Margaret Grant. Yes, there is a lot of course content to cover and there are a few readings each week, but if you engage with the material it is straightforward and enjoyable. I liked this format of hybrid teaching. Lectures were great. Assignments and tests not too difficult. Highly recommend taking a course with her.

her lectures were confusing, with her slides being full of words but no clear indication to what we were learning. this was a hybrid class, so we we were required to do 4/5 participation assignments plus "an hour" of extra reading outside of lectures. she focused on linguistics more than cognitive science. seems nice, but not the best prof.

Awful lecturer who's main focus was on the linguistics side of COGS and hardly anything else. Gave out way too many readings to fill in the 1 hour of designated online time for the course. Made 0 effort to prepare her students for any exams and never responded to emails/canvas messages. Took as an elective yet is somehow my lowest grade. Avoid her.

Way too much work for a base 100 level class. 70+ slides every plus readings plus extra videos to watch every single week. Midterms and final are extremely tough, asking very specific questions, including who says... and minute details that come from her 1000+ slides. Not a good class for an introduction to cognitive science nor for GPA boost.

The lectures were dry and she covered a lot of material very quickly but then in the exams, the questions were more detailed. There were two midterms and one cumulative final. The course didn't feel like a 100-level course due to tough exams.

The problem was that Margaret specializes in linguistics, so the content tended to be more about the linguistic side of cognitive science. I'm not sure how much she really knew about the other topics in cognitive science but I was still able to learn a lot. She also seemed a bit unorganized at times but she was caring towards the students.
there was a LOT of content covered in this class. but she explained and also had lecture recordings. make sure you prepare your written questions well for the exam. good luck
